title: New strategy hypothesis selection memo (comparison only)

donchian.s23: WATCH (positive IS+OOS but OOS only 16 trades; not deployable)
donchian.s24: ENTRY_EDGE_NOT_SUPPORTED (raw breakout entry no edge vs random darts)
donchian.s25: SEQUENCE_RISK_FRAGILE (IS bootstrap P(<=0)=20.9%; removing top 3 flips IS -3.82R and OOS -1.93R negative; OOS ~50% one trade)

s26_trend_sr_ema_rsi.notes: OOS PASS both primaries but ENTRY_EDGE_INCONCLUSIVE, PARTIAL_SUPPORT_WITH_CAUTION, REGIME_RISK_INCONCLUSIVE, WALK_FORWARD_INCONCLUSIVE, FRICTION_SENSITIVE; thin IS edge (PF~1.20, exp~0.065R); whole record post-2016; 2014-2016 NQ+ES collapse; OOS single-year-dominated

s27_mean_reversion_bull.notes: IS 2013-2022: 18 trades, -1.7506R, PF 0.8372, expectancy -0.097R, 3/10 positive years, anti-Donchian top-3 gate FAIL (-6.25R ex top3); capped +1.5R target rarely reached, two-step confirmation too restrictive; no OOS used

must_avoid.weak_or_inconclusive_entry_edge: Donchian (NOT_SUPPORTED) and S26 (INCONCLUSIVE) both failed to show the entry beats random-in-regime. The next entry must be a discrete, separable event that CAN be significance-tested AND must actually pass before any continue.
must_avoid.thin_or_negative_is_expectancy: S26 ~0.065R; S27 negative. Need real per-trade headroom, not a marginal edge.
must_avoid.high_friction_sensitivity: S26 IS went negative at 0.10R/trade. Prefer fewer, larger-R trades with friction headroom.
must_avoid.regime_concentration_2014_2016: The ONE failure shared by Donchian and S26 is the 2014-2016 choppy-regime collapse; both lived entirely in the post-2016 trend. The next idea must either be counter-cyclical OR carry an explicit mechanical no-trade regime gate.
must_avoid.top_winner_dependence: Donchian net flips negative without its top 3 trades. Avoid fat-tail-winner dependence; per-trade outcomes should be repeatable.
must_avoid.low_trade_count: Donchian OOS 16, S27 IS 18. Over-restrictive multi-step confirmations starve the sample. Keep the entry selective but not rare.

data_reality.implication: Daily OHLCV including real volume is available for NQ and ES with no new fetch. Any volume- or ATR-expansion proxy is testable immediately. Intraday or a new underlying would need a separately-authorized data fetch.

candidates.name: Volatility-confirmed trend continuation
candidates.hypothesis: In an uptrend, enter continuation only after a volatility/range expansion confirms the move's direction, on the thesis that expansion-backed continuations persist.
candidates.why_real_entry_edge: An expansion gate could in principle separate real continuations from drift.
candidates.avoids_prior_weaknesses: Bigger moves give some friction headroom vs S26.
candidates.reintroduces_prior_weaknesses: Same trend-continuation family as the parked S26; the entry is again hard to separate from the trend filter and it would collapse in the same 2014-2016 chop.
candidates.regime_risk: HIGH -- identical trend dependence and 2014-2016 vulnerability to S26
candidates.biggest_failure_mode: Re-discovers S26: entry significance inconclusive again, 2014-2016 collapse again.
candidates.first_clean_test_design: Daily NQ IS 2013-2022: continuation entries WITH vs WITHOUT the expansion gate vs random-in-uptrend, to isolate the gate's marginal edge.
candidates.entry_significance_cleanly_testable: yes (vs random-in-regime), but expected to be inconclusive like S26
candidates.verdict: WEAK -- closest to a parked family; least new information.

candidates.name: Breakout-retest with volatility-expansion confirmation (+ mechanical bad-regime no-trade gate)
candidates.hypothesis: Wait for a clean break of a prior daily resistance level; do NOT enter the breakout. Enter only on a subsequent RETEST that HOLDS the broken level (prior resistance becomes support), and only when a volatility/volume expansion confirms participation. Skip everything in a mechanically-flagged low-vol/chop regime.
candidates.why_real_entry_edge: The retest-that-holds is a discrete, datable, behaviorally-grounded event (trapped breakout sellers + new demand at the reclaimed shelf) that is structurally DISTINCT from both Donchian's raw breakout and S26's pullback-in-uptrend. Its marginal value can be measured directly against entering on the raw breakout.
candidates.avoids_prior_weaknesses: Distinct entry event from both parked families; the retest filter is designed to reject the false breakouts that define chop; the vol/volume-expansion gate yields fewer, larger-R trades (friction headroom) and is computable from real daily volume already present; an explicit no-trade regime gate folds in candidate 4's insight to attack the 2014-2016 collapse head-on.
candidates.reintroduces_prior_weaknesses: Still trend-following in spirit, so a pure chop regime is dangerous -- which is precisely why the vol-expansion gate and the bad-regime no-trade filter are part of the hypothesis, not optional add-ons.
candidates.friction_sensitivity: low-moderate -- wider stops on larger structures give good headroom
candidates.regime_risk: MEDIUM -- chop is the native risk, but the retest filter + vol gate + explicit no-trade regime gate directly target it (vs S26 which had no such gate)
candidates.multi_market_testability: EXCELLENT -- daily NQ + independent ES (plus MNQ/MES corroboration), no new data
candidates.data_requirements: none new (daily OHLCV incl. real volume already provisioned)
candidates.biggest_failure_mode: Choppy regime still generates failed retests; mitigated by the vol gate + no-trade regime filter and bounded by an R-stop. If the retest does not beat raw-breakout entry in significance testing, PARK immediately (do not repeat S26's 'inconclusive but continue').
candidates.first_clean_test_design: Daily NQ IS 2013-2022, FROZEN before any OOS: mechanical level definition (e.g. prior swing high / N-day high), breakout rule, retest-hold rule, vol/volume-expansion gate, no-trade regime gate, R-stop and R-target. THEN run TWO significance tests: (a) retest entries vs random-in-uptrend; (b) retest entries vs entering on the raw breakout -- isolating the retest's marginal edge AND producing information about the parked Donchian. Only after IS is frozen, evaluate ES independently and 2023-2025 OOS.
candidates.entry_significance_cleanly_testable: yes -- and uniquely it supports a SECOND test (retest vs raw-breakout) that isolates the new component's marginal value
candidates.verdict: STRONGEST -- separable & double-testable entry event, distinct from parked families, friction headroom, daily independent NQ/ES, with regime mitigation built into the hypothesis.

candidates.name: Pullback with market confirmation preserving independent validation
candidates.hypothesis: Enter a pullback in an uptrend that is confirmed by a market signal computed from NQ ALONE; only LATER validate on ES independently, never folding ES into the NQ entry.
candidates.why_real_entry_edge: NQ-internal confirmation could filter low-quality pullbacks while keeping ES as a clean independent validator.
candidates.avoids_prior_weaknesses: Explicitly fixes the S27-D1 Idea-3 flaw (it does NOT consume ES as the independent market), preserving multi-market validation.
candidates.reintroduces_prior_weaknesses: Mechanism is a pullback-in-uptrend -- the SAME family as the parked S26. High risk of re-discovering S26's inconclusive entry; the NQ-only 'confirmation' is unlikely to add separable edge S26 already could not show.
candidates.multi_market_testability: good and methodologically clean (ES kept independent)
candidates.biggest_failure_mode: Collapses back into S26: entry significance inconclusive again.
candidates.first_clean_test_design: Daily NQ IS 2013-2022: pullback entries WITH vs WITHOUT the NQ-internal confirmation vs random-in-uptrend; only if the confirmation adds clear edge, validate on ES.
candidates.entry_significance_cleanly_testable: yes, but likely inconclusive given S26 precedent
candidates.verdict: MODERATE -- methodologically clean but too close to the parked S26.

candidates.name: Bad-regime avoidance / no-trade classifier
candidates.hypothesis: Not a new entry. Build a mechanical, pre-registered daily classifier that flags 'favorable' vs 'chop/bad' regime (especially 2014-2016-style), and trade only in favorable regime.
candidates.why_real_entry_edge: It carries NO entry edge of its own -- by construction it produces no trades; its value is conditional on an underlying entry that already has edge.
candidates.avoids_prior_weaknesses: Directly targets the ONE failure shared by every parked branch -- the 2014-2016 choppy-regime collapse -- rather than hoping a filter incidentally helps.
candidates.reintroduces_prior_weaknesses: Regime classifiers are easy to overfit; and gating an edgeless entry (S26/S27) creates no edge.
candidates.multi_market_testability: good -- a regime label is computable and persistence-testable on daily NQ and ES independently, read-only, without touching S26/S27
candidates.biggest_failure_mode: Produces nothing tradeable on its own; best used as a gate folded INTO a chosen entry (which is exactly what the winner does).
candidates.first_clean_test_design: Define the regime label mechanically; test on daily NQ/ES whether the label is separable and persistent, and (read-only, descriptively) whether favorable-regime gating would have changed the parked branches' committed trade lists.
candidates.verdict: STRONG INSIGHT, NOT A STANDALONE BRANCH -- its core idea is absorbed into the winner as a no-trade regime gate.

candidates.name: Time-based / seasonal tendency with trend filter
candidates.hypothesis: Enter only during a pre-registered calendar window (e.g. a month/turn-of-month/day-of-week tendency) while a trend filter holds.
candidates.why_real_entry_edge: Some calendar tendencies have weak documented persistence; combined with a trend gate they could be tradeable.
candidates.avoids_prior_weaknesses: Distinct mechanism from all parked families; trivially significance-testable (window vs out-of-window).
candidates.reintroduces_prior_weaknesses: Weak theoretical edge; calendar effects are notoriously regime-dependent and prone to spurious discovery; narrow windows starve the trade count.
candidates.expected_trade_count: low (narrow windows) -- risks the S27/Donchian low-count problem
candidates.regime_risk: HIGH -- seasonal effects decay and flip across regimes
candidates.biggest_failure_mode: Spurious seasonal artifact that does not generalize OOS or to ES.
candidates.first_clean_test_design: Daily NQ IS 2013-2022: pre-register the window BEFORE looking; in-window vs out-of-window returns vs random calendar windows; validate on ES.
candidates.entry_significance_cleanly_testable: yes, cleanly -- but high spurious-discovery risk
candidates.verdict: WEAK-MODERATE -- clean to test but weak prior and overfit-prone.

selected_hypothesis.name: Breakout-retest with volatility-expansion confirmation + mechanical bad-regime no-trade gate
selected_hypothesis.why_selected: It is the only candidate that pairs a DISCRETE, separable, and DOUBLE-testable entry event (retest-that-holds, testable both vs random-in-regime AND vs raw-breakout entry) with built-in regime mitigation. The retest entry is structurally distinct from both parked families (Donchian = raw breakout; S26 = pullback-in-uptrend), giving genuinely new information rather than re-discovery. It uses real daily volume already provisioned (no fetch), offers friction headroom via fewer/larger-R trades, and is immediately validatable on NQ and independent ES. Crucially, it absorbs candidate 4's core insight as a hard no-trade regime gate, attacking the 2014-2016 chop collapse that sank Donchian and S26 -- the one failure none of the prior entry redesigns escaped.
key_risks: Chop is still the native risk of a trend-following structure; the vol-expansion gate and no-trade regime filter must do real work, not cosmetic filtering.
Overfitting the level/breakout/retest/vol-gate definitions -- all must be frozen before any result, with no sweeps (the S26/S27 anti-overfit discipline).
Trade-count starvation if the retest + vol gate + regime gate stack too restrictively (watch the Donchian/S27 low-count failure).
Entry-significance must actually PASS: if the retest does not beat raw-breakout entry, PARK immediately rather than continuing 'inconclusive' like S26.
selected_hypothesis.first_s28_d2_spec_direction: Pre-register a FROZEN spec on daily NQ IS 2013-2022 (OOS 2023-2025 sealed): (1) mechanical resistance level (e.g. prior N-day high / confirmed swing high); (2) breakout rule (close beyond level by a fixed buffer); (3) retest-hold rule (price returns to the level within a fixed window and closes back above it); (4) volatility/volume-expansion gate using ATR and the real daily volume column; (5) a mechanical bad-regime NO-TRADE gate (e.g. trend-vs-chop label) defined before any result; (6) fixed R-stop and capped R-target with conservative same-bar precedence; (7) one position at a time, R-only. Pre-register BOTH significance tests (retest vs random-in-regime; retest vs raw-breakout) and the full pass/watch/fail ladder BEFORE any IS run. No optimization; spec frozen before results.

guardrails.note: This memo selects a direction only. It authorizes no code, no backtest, no data fetch. S28-D2 (spec pre-registration) is a separate, to-be-authorized step.

final_line: S28-D1 is hypothesis selection only; no strategy code, backtest, OOS, paper, or live trading is authorized.
trading_recommendation: NONE. Research-direction memo only. Donchian, S26, and S27 stay PARKED; no new strategy is validated.
